reviews.review_description:
I played this game about 9 years ago on the Oculus Rift when it was free, and had a lot of fun. I beat all the single player campaigns (good luck doing that now) my only complaint was the multiplayer problems and half the factions locked behind a paywall, but since it was free then it was understandable.

 It was hard but fair, and you could progress without having to spend money, but that is no longer the case unless you just want to sit on multiplayer with your starter decks getting pounded all day collecting rewards for losing

 Game is similar to Magic the Gathering with four lanes where you move forward until you get to the enemy side and finally attack their life total. The developers did a good job of making a game that was clever and fun, while the meta monetization arm did a good job of utterly destroying it with their greed.

 You will get ONE booster with a few cards in it ONE time per week IF you log on every single day. For all the times I leveled up I don't think it ever gave me anything at all like it used to, never a single card. With that lack of progression, I have opened only  two boosters in all the time I have played this game after returning, about 20 games after the long tutorial, which also gave me nothing. Every faction you complete should give you something, what a ridiculous reward system you guys have in this game now. First booster had  completely useless common stuff, the other ....

 I finally won enough matches against overpowered AI decks in the campaign (Who start with a huge Advantages like pre-built forts or pre-existing cards in play that get stronger every turn, or you have ridiculously short timer while AI drags its turn out forever)  my reward, ...one booster pack. At least this one had a nice leader unit, woohoo! Lets go put that into a deck... 

This is when I find out the only good card this game ever gave me went to one of the four factions you have to pay extra money to be able to play, after buying this game that is no longer free, but still full of just as many micro purchases.   

DEVS....

Why would I spend money on this title or any other tcg when the old and identical game is no longer playable, making all those cards and all those purchases completely unusable and a waste of money? Could have let us continue to play the old game hosting our own servers, instead you shut it down entirely.

You pulled servers, re-released the same exact title after turning up difficulty and down rewards, and expect us all to start over and rebuy everything just like HEX did after account wipes. 

Those two games show everyone that all these purchases in trading card games are a bad investment. They are really just things you rented and can use up until they shut down their servers, again.

/Uninstall

reviews.review_title: Started great but now not enjoyable
reviews.review_description:
Ai just make it too difficult to enjoy a reasonable game, i dont  want to put 100 hours in just to win one campaign.......
this was a waste

I didn't expect this scam, their advertising is nothing like the real game, it's a total pay to win game from the start, you don't even need tactics to win if you have good cards, I spent $ and started getting some wins but it's boring so I don't recommend this game at all, the match making is way tooo unbalanced. I tried it two weeks and already uninstalled it. Don't waste money buying it or in game boosters

reviews.review_title: Amazing card game! 100% NOT pay to win!
reviews.review_description:
Alot of negative reviews don’t know how to play and probably can’t beat the story campaign!!

This is an excellent card strategy game! Most of those who say pay to win just don’t grasp the “chess-like” aspect of the game. If you have played card games like “magic the gathering” or yugioh” you will feel right at home! There are card packs that you can buy if you are impatient but by no means is that the only way to get great cards or just the cards you want. 

Definitely give it a try especially if you like card games!!

Anyone having trouble after downloading, feel free to add me and I’ll show you the ropes!! 
😁👍🏾👍🏾👍🏾
I've played dragon front since the Samsung gear vr and Facebook gamesroom , I'm very happy to play it again on Q3

Total false advertising, this game is utterly pay to win, no matter how skilled you are you will get to a point pretty early in the game where the ai will just let lose op troops on you and will annihilate you, don’t waste your time, how this game has been allowed to stay up and advertise on the store is absolutely scandalous.
I’d give it a zero star if I could, be warned.

If you want to get ahead you will need to sink a lot of money on to this game.

Many in-app purchases to buy the required boosts and cards.

It takes more than the minimum time for a valid refund to find that this is a purcharge game. Shame on you Meta.

I played this game some years ago, was perfect gameplay and balanced AL.
 Now it's a disaster,  it's became disaster after Meta pucharged this game.

That is fkd up now:
 - Meta lying about about human player, then we to multiplayer.  It's all AL.
 - Dumb AL power settings at lev4. The only way you can win is to buy packs. Meta killed this game.
 - No offline mode.

I noticed....games owned by Meta are only about pucharging game packs and spending money.
They are killing joy and fun if VR games.
I love this game. Making decks is the key to beating campaigns.

UPDATE: 06 OCT 2024
After several months, the ‘invite friends’ option is still broken! The devs haven’t fixed it in all this time. Still can’t play with friends. Game is broken. DO NOT BUY. 

The tutorial is so long that by the time you get through it, you’ll have exceeded the time limit to return the game. There are 8 factions, each with a different starting deck of cards, so you are immediately thrown in the deep end trying to figure out >50 units and how each faction works, including mechanics like death blow, flight, etc. You muddle through the tutorial largely by just winging it, figuring it’ll start to click once you’re actually familiar with the units. 

After a few hours playing the tutorial, my friends and I just wanted to play together. The MULTIPLAYER FRIEND INVITE IS INCREDIBLY BUGGY. It took more than 15 minutes and several invite tries from both of us before the broken friend multiplayer finally worked and actually put us in a battle together. 

We FINALLY got to play but suddenly found there’s a time limit to each turn. Only there’s no clock positioned intuitively anywhere to show you how much time you have left. There were no settings for us to change or turn off the time limit. We needed more time as we were still trying to learn all the units. The way the game mechanics work, especially around mana, the time limit made the game broken and unplayable in any practical sense. 

This game concept had potential, but extremely poor execution makes it not worth bothering. Given the P2W component on top of it, I strongly recommend against this game. 

The devs need to
1. Implement an adjustable time setting, so that new players can play together while still learning all the units 
2. Introduce a clock in an obvious place, in the game environment, so you know how much time you have left. 
3. Fix the bugs in the friend multiplayer invite system. 

As is, the game is effectively broken and unplayable for new players who want to play together.

reviews.review_title: 10/10  A fantastic card game like MtG
reviews.review_description: So I don’t leave many reviews, but this game deserves one. Me and my sons are all big fans of Yugioh and Magic the Gathering, especially the Commander style of play. This game is very reminiscent of that, and is super easy and quick to understand. Each deck/faction feels unique and fun, and the ability to customize your decks extends the fun. Definitely recommend.

I had hesitated to get this game as Cards and Tankards scratches the itch, but I saw it was half off and figured I'd give it a go.  It's a cool game, but I couldn't find anyone in online play.  I would LOVE to do the campaign, but the difficulty everyone has mentioned is real.  I thought they nerfed it, but playing against the AI is ridiculous and after a while frustrating. I've quit a number of times. I like a challenge, but come on now...this is the same issue I have with Demeo.  Hopefully they work on the difficulty more...otherwise this will end up in the company of uninstalled regret purchases on my Quest.

Poor monetization scheme. You pay for the game and then pay for in-game items. Very pay to win. You can earn stuff, but incredibly slowly. There's a daily/weekly quest system, but it's designed so obtusely that it's easy to fail and have all your progress deleted. It all feels very greedy on the part of the developers.

Decent strategy game otherwise. But in the bigger picture, this is a game that already failed once. It came out years ago and was shut down because no one played it. Now, they've revitalized it and it looks like there's still nobody playing it. So you risk spending money on a game that will just be shut down again, or at the very least never release any new content.

Definitely my type of game. It is extremely fun and yes very very challenging which in my book is a great plus. It doesn’t feel forced to buy booster packs and with every ranked multiplayer win you get a booster pack. Playing online is definitely easier than the conquering mode. I like the different art style and graphics too.
Such a good game at a price point of only 10€!

You have to like challenging games to like this one.

developer_response.body:
Hey MuffinBulldog. Love to hear you're enjoying the game and embracing the challenge! Thanks for taking the time to leave your review and thoughts.

See you on the battlefield!

reviews.review_description:
The app got thousands of reviews for years on Oculus Rift and Oulus go before they stopped and have changed the platform to Meta quest. I love the game soooo much 😍🥰. Thank you for coming back. It's one of the best board games in the VR world 🌎 I often end up playing it for hours every day, lol 😆
If you like or want to develop your strategies, logical, patient,... skills, it's your must game 🎮 
-->New players hint: it took me about a half year to learn most of the tricks to beat other players. Warning: Once you get it, you may get addicted to the game too much 😅

Thanks for your tips and thoughts, and hope to see you on the battlefield!

reviews.review_description: Tutorial is fun, but even single player campaign begs for your money... unless you have infinite patience. Doesn't even worth the 10 euros i bought it. Better free games out there. Don't waste your money and most importantly don't break your nerves.

developer_response.body:
Hey there anxis411. Appreciate you leaving your review and thoughts about the game. 

There are actually a handful of intended ways to earn Booster Packs just by playing the game!

1. Leveling up different factions by earning exp. This can be done in Conquest and Ranked quite quickly, regardless of if you win or lose! Gold and Boosters are awarded as you level up, so make sure you switch between factions as you play!

2. Earning gold in multiplayer to purchase booster packs. Gold can be earned by playing Ranked matches, regardless of winning or losing! Also completing your ranked challenges awards gold, and Dualinium, that can be used to purchase booster packs and craft powerful Dual Champions. Don't shy away from multiplayer!

3. After you try the above to help craft more powerful decks, jump back into Conquest, where you earn Booster packs for completing campaigns, which will help snowball your decks even further!

If you want to chat deckbuilding and strategy with other players, check out the Dragon Front discord! https://discord.gg/XnayNQBC

Hope this helps, and that you can experience the fun you had in the tutorial in the rest of the game as well!

reviews.review_description: I love this game it’s great fun to play and there all ways seem to be players to match make with the longest I have waited is just under 3 minutes and most of the time even quicker. This game really reminds me of a game called eye of judgement which was a great game using real card and computers to mix the world together and this game does the same thing but so much better by using mixed reality. It brings the whole world into our house and it feels great. When people use spell and magic you so it all around you and seeing portals opening your ceiling amazing. I do see what some people are saying about how the graphics change when you look at different points of the area. Lots of games use this method to achieve hi resolution. I don’t know if frame generation maybe better way to go only a idea. The game is hard but does get easier the more you play and understand the cards you are using. The key is don’t be afraid to play other people it is ranked matched based so some time it is best to play against a new players which are human and on the same level you are at. Remember even if you lose you still get experience and gold towards booster pack. By completing other tasks this will help you unlock other stuff. You can pay money but you don’t have to, I have played people with better decks and me and still won. Worth a look if you like any of those kind of card games like magic ect I would recommend this game.

developer_response.body: Hey wolfman6001! Thanks much for taking the time to leave your extensive thoughts and review. Appreciate your feedback on visuals and gameplay, and glad you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_description: Get this u need to win to innocent anything without spending money. Well the conquest mode is very hard with no way to make it easier or Hardee. Since you cant win you can't progress and get better decks. It's a joke amd the devs clearly dont care about it

developer_response.body:
Hey there FaizedFox. Thanks for taking the time to leave your feedback. Conquest is definitely made with challenge in mind, and a place to truly test the deck crafting ability of the players.

That said, there are a handful of ways to earn booster packs that will help you build your decks up fast!

1. Leveling up different factions by earning exp. This can be done in Conquest and Ranked quite quickly, regardless of if you win or lose! Gold and Boosters are awarded as you level up, so make sure you switch between factions as you play!

2. Earning gold to purchase booster packs. Gold can be earned by playing Ranked matches, regardless of winning or losing! Also completing your ranked challenges awards gold, and Dualinium, that can be used to purchase booster packs and craft powerful Dual Champions!

3. After you try the above to help craft more powerful decks, jump back into Conquest, where you earn Booster packs for completing campaigns, which will help snowball your decks even further!

If you want to chat deckbuilding and strategy with other players, check out the Dragon Front discord! https://discord.gg/XnayNQBC

Consider the above and you'll be conquering Conquest mode before you know it.

reviews.review_description:
I like this game, I was done all campaign in oculus rift s version.. but this New quest version is too difficult in solo/ campaign mode. The goal? ..buy extra deck ? It s sade.. please, make many difficulty modes to choose or set the older ia version (rift).
Thanks, I love this Nice game.. but more playable please..

developer_response.body:
Hey there raph_la_menace. Thanks for leaving the rating and feedback. 

In our recent update that came out today, we actually nerfed a few of the more difficult conquest missions, as we felt they were a bit too challenging.

The best way to earn new cards is always to jump into ranked multiplayer to work on completing quests, and collecting gold at match end, regardless of winning or losing! Then that gold can be used to earn card packs in the shop.

Glad you are enjoying the game, and hope to see you on the battlefield!

reviews.review_description: P2W, half of the game hidden behind paywall, game is asking for more money, the real price is several times higher.

First off, I love card games and VR. The mechanics are simple to understand, but quite complex when exploring the huge card pool. Spells, units, forts, and champions bring an exciting element to the game and keep you busy building different type of decks.  The characters  and lore is excellent. Best way to describe it is a fantasy / steam punk atmosphere.  Mix Reality is done well and it’s my favorite way to play the game.  The sounds and music are top notch, and the animations are great.  

There can be a huge skill gap in PVP. Play the game, collect more cards and slowly you will improve.  I’m sure there will be reviews giving dragon front 1 star and accused it for being P2W, but the decks you start with are not terrible. Making smart decisions and having game knowledge is what will make you a top player. 

There are many factions and all play very unique.   The community on discord is very small, but friendly and helpful.  I recommend you join it. You can ask questions, give feedback, and arrange games with other players if it’s a slow day on rank.

developer_response.body: Hey DescryGaming, thanks for being part of the community, and for the great content and feedback you provide for the team and playerbase. We hope you continue to enjoy the game as we work to continue to improve it!

reviews.review_description: I am so glad they released this game for Quest.  I use to play this on the original CV1.  It is easy to pickup and there are good players to play against at the top levels as well. Solo and multiplayer are fun.  This game is super addictive!

We hope that our future content continues to delight!

developer_response.body: Hey DoctaShaggy! Glad to hear you're enjoying the game, and thank you for the feedback about the controllers. Cheers!

reviews.review_title: Solid VR title, could use some improveme
reviews.review_description:
This review will be edited in a lengthy, more detailed review over time. For now, I have played for about 2 hours and barely scratched what the game has to offer.

The factions are nice, feel balanced so far, and have very different cards and feel to them. Unlocking boosters is a fun grinding experience.

The game is quite hard to master. The solo experience is challenging but will get you ready to face real players.

My main complaint comes with the MR mode. There are no reasons not to be able to play the entire game in mixed reality. That is the kind of game I play seated, on the sofa... While watching TV or keeping an eye on the dogs. MR is perfect for that! But it only happens during rounds. As soon as you finish playing a round, you're sent back in VR facing point to click menus with an environment surrounding you. It brings nothing to the game and imo, should be MR all the way.

The game also uses a UI that is point to click. The menu could easily be brought closer to the user and use a touch to click menu like Demeo for example. That means hands tracking would be an option for them to consider. Controllers are fine, but when you want to sit back, relax, play a few rounds with a cup of coffee... It simply won't do it.

With that said, the pros outweigh the cons. The cons are very specific to some use cases that may not affect most players and the overall experience is extremely enjoyable.

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to leave your review mikael.dufresne! Your feedback on MR has been heard, and it's great to hear you're enjoying the game overall.

reviews.review_description:
I purchased this because I like card games. Even though I do not understand the rules fully yet, I still had fun. It's a steep learning curve and hopefully I will get better. Would be a five star review but at the moment I will give it a three-star

Hey Mobey561! Thanks for taking the time to leave a review and your thoughts on the game. 

If you want to chat deckbuilding and strategy with other players, check out the Dragon Front discord! https://discord.gg/XnayNQBC

I find it worth mentioning that Conquest is definitely meant to be a challenge! If you're looking to master the basics, give the various tutorials a try for each faction, as well as practice mode.

Also want to mention some helpful ways to quickly earn more cards and make your decks more powerful!:

1. Leveling up different factions by earning exp. This can be done in Conquest and Ranked quite quickly, regardless of if you win or lose! Gold and Boosters are awarded as you level up, so make sure you switch between factions as you play!

2. Earning gold to purchase booster packs. Gold can be earned by playing Ranked matches, regardless of winning or losing! Also completing your ranked challenges awards gold, and Dualinium, that can be used to purchase booster packs and craft powerful Dual Champions!

3. After you try the above to help craft more powerful decks, jump back into Conquest, where you earn Booster packs for completing campaigns, which will help snowball your decks even further!

Hope this helps, and that your mastery of the game leads to an even better experience for you!

developer_response.body:
Hey there darkstar12345, thanks for taking the time to leave your review.

The low resolution/extreme pixelation you're experiencing is definitely unintended, often seen when using the Quest Pro and its Eye Tracking feature. A fix for this issue is in progress.

We hope to resolve the inconsistencies here so that you and other players can focus on the gameplay without any visual distractions.

We hope that future content will continue to improve the experience for you!

I have all but the first 3 Decks and cannot get the decks either for some reason.  Anyone having this issue as well?  How do I correct this issue?  Thorns-Eclipse-Scales are the Decks I cannot seem to get.  Once I have those then I will have all of the decks and I can rate this game as a 5-Star.

developer_response.body: GammaSenior, really glad to hear you're enjoying the game. Thank you for leaving your review and feedback on your experience thus far! See you on the battlefield!

reviews.review_description:
The short and simple is, if you've ever been hooked on grid-based strategy games like Fire Emblem, FFT, etc., you'll get your fix here. 

The aesthetic feels very Warcraft with race inspiration from there to Starcraft to DnD and beyond, but with a nice bit of background lore for each to make them feel unique.  The first tutorial is comprehensive and straight-forward, as it walks you through each potential action and the reasons you might do each for any given situation.  Ranked can sound intimidating, but you'll likely start off facing others as new and nervous as yourself.  I won my first two online matches with only the base Eclipse deck, and each victory granted me a booster pack of 4.  You'll start off with the base decks of four races ; the other four can be worked towards via daily rewards or paid for. 

I did run into a wall in Conquest mode ; I was not expecting that immediate difficulty curve (or cliff) from Mission 1 of each.  Still, as both other reviews and the developers have mentioned, you'll find others of equal level in multiplayer and a more manageable challenge for your daily rewards there.  Yes, maybe there could be a few whale$ out there, but they won't be sticking around the early ranks long enough to worry about. It did not occur to me to try multiplayer first, so I can see why some others walked away frustrated after only trying the tutorial before jumping straight into the harshness of Conquest.

My only nitpicking is I find the monetization is a bit odd.  Typically, digital CCGs are free-to-play if they also involve paid-for booster packs.  Still, the rewards system is generous enough with a bit of patience.  Now that I've finally made some progress in the game by trying multiplayer, I can see myself sinking into this for years to come.

developer_response.body: Hey toxicfoxnic! Glad you're enjoying the game, and really appreciate your in depth review and feedback on cards, conquest and monetization. Hope to see you out there on the battlefield!

reviews.review_description:
Most negative things:

1) awfully slow AI in single player. The AI needs minutes to do actions and end the turn. especially if it pumps the mana to the hero, it looks like it wana make you suffer by doing it once every 5s. there is one level where you need to end turn in 10s while the AI takes minutes...

2) UI is totally unusable. you need to grab the handles at the table to come step by step closer to the table. this is absolutely unpractical so you can't  enjoy the details of the creatures by zooming in. also because you have no options to tilt the table you need always look down which makes your neck hurt after a while. demeo is a positive example how to do it right.

3) graphic performance breaks down after the board is half full with creatures. you can't read the text of the cards anymore and the creature's looks pixeled.

conclusion:
It looks like the developers spend 99% of the budget for character details and animation and 1% to develop a fun game experience.

developer_response.body:
Hey there w0lt3r, we really appreciate the feedback you've provided.

Regarding your thoughts on the slow AI and the graphical performance issues, these are high priority issues that are currently being worked on by the team. Similarly, the extreme pixelation being seen as the board fills is unintended, notable especially when using the Quest Pro and its Eye Tracking feature. A fix is in progress here as well.

As for the UI feedback, this is really valuable as there may be a targeted improvement we can make, so thanks for pointing it out.

We hope to make improvements where possible to exceed your expectations in the future!
